mechanism: Sucrose (and its hydrolytic hexose products glucose/fructose) represses MIR156A and MIR156C gene transcription, reducing mature miR156 levels to promote juvenile-to-adult transition
miR156 targets SPL9 mRNA for cleavage and translational repression; high miR156 in juvenile phase keeps SPL9 levels low

SPL9 promotes floral transition and shoot maturation; higher SPL9 activity leads to earlier flowering and shorter inflorescence stems. spl9 spl15 double mutant showed significantly reduced inflorescence height (6.9 cm vs 12.1 cm in wild-type) demonstrating SPL9 shortens plant height via earlier reproductive transition
Extended cold exposure progressively induces VIN3 transcription via unknown cold sensors
VIN3 PHD protein joins PRC2-like complex to deposit H3K27me3 at FLC and silence it
FRI (FRIGIDA) is a coiled-coil protein that recruits chromatin modifiers to activate FLC expression to high levels
FLC (MADS-box transcription factor) directly represses FT expression by binding to its promoter/regulatory regions, delaying flowering
Red light converts phyB to active Pfr form; far-red converts back to inactive Pr
Low R:FR (shade) transcriptionally induces HFR1 via phytochromes as a negative-feedback brake
Light-activated phyB binds PIF4 to promote its phosphorylation-dependent degradation
HFR1 forms non-DNA-binding heterodimers with PIF4, blocking PIF4 DNA binding without degradation
PAR1 small HLH prevents PIF4 from binding DNA
High ambient temperature increases PIF4 protein abundance and activity, independent of light
GA 20-oxidase 1 catalyzes the oxidation of GA53/GA12 through GA44/GA15 and GA19/GA24 to GA20/GA9, a key late step in bioactive GA synthesis
GA 3-oxidase 1 (GA4) catalyzes the final step converting GA9/GA20 to bioactive GA4/GA1
GA 2-oxidase 1 inactivates bioactive GAs by 2-beta hydroxylation; reduces GA pool

Bioactive GA upregulates GA2ox1 transcription, promoting its own catabolism (feedforward/homeostasis) [Framework note: this edge bypasses a receptor Perception Gate (Motif 1). Biologically the feedback runs through DELLA/ARF/ABI5 but those intermediate edges are absent from curated_edges.json. Exogenous-hormone in receptor-KO backgrounds will still modulate this target - a known Trap-5 framework limitation.]
Bioactive GA binds the GID1A receptor, causing conformational change and recruitment of DELLA proteins
GA-bound GID1A physically interacts with DELLA protein RGA, promoting its recognition by SCF-SLY1 and degradation via the 26S proteasome
SLY1 is the F-box subunit of an SCF E3 ubiquitin ligase that polyubiquitinates DELLA proteins including RGA, targeting them for 26S proteasome degradation
DELLA RGA physically interacts with PIF4 and prevents it from binding target promoters, limiting PIF4-driven growth genes
DELLA RGA binds BZR1 to prevent BZR1 DNA binding, integrating GA and BR signaling at target genes
DELLA RGA binds ARF6, inhibiting auxin-driven elongation gene expression
DELLA protein RGA represses cell elongation in stem; rga loss-of-function rescues ga1-3 dwarf phenotype; RGA is the principal DELLA limiting vegetative growth
DWF4 (CYP90B1) C-22 hydroxylase catalyzes the rate-limiting step in BR biosynthesis
Active BZR1 binds BRRE element in DWF4 promoter and represses BR biosynthesis (feedback homeostasis)
Brassinolide binds BRI1 leucine-rich repeat receptor kinase ectodomain as a molecular glue, triggering BRI1-BAK1 heterodimerization
BRI1-BAK1 complex phosphorylates and activates BSK1 (BR signaling kinase)
BRI1-BAK1 complex phosphorylates and activates BSK1 (BR signaling kinase)
BSU1 dephosphorylates BIN2 at pTyr200, inactivating the kinase
BIN2 (a GSK3-like kinase) phosphorylates BZR1, promoting cytoplasmic retention by 14-3-3 proteins and inactivating its DNA binding
Dephosphorylated/active BZR1 in nucleus activates BR-responsive cell elongation genes; bzr1-D constitutively active mutant promotes growth
YUC8 (flavin monooxygenase) converts indole-3-pyruvate to IAA (auxin) in the YUC-dependent tryptophan pathway
PIF4 directly binds the YUC8 promoter and activates transcription to drive auxin biosynthesis
PIF4 activates GA20ox1 transcription to raise GA levels in shade and high temperature
Auxin binds TIR1 F-box co-receptor, promoting TIR1 interaction with Aux/IAA repressors and their ubiquitination
SCF-TIR1 ubiquitinates auxin-bound Aux/IAA19 for 26S proteasome degradation, releasing ARF6/7/8 to activate elongation genes
Aux/IAA IAA19 binds ARF7 transcription factor and represses ARF activity
ARF6 activates auxin-responsive elongation genes in hypocotyl and stem
IPT3 (ATP/ADP isopentenyltransferase 3) is a major CK biosynthesis enzyme producing isopentenyladenine riboside 5'-phosphate from DMAPP and AMP/ADP
CKX3 (cytokinin oxidase 3) degrades cytokinins; feedback-induced by CK itself

Auxin represses IPT3 transcription, linking auxin to CK depletion in axillary buds [Framework note: this edge bypasses a receptor Perception Gate (Motif 1). Biologically the feedback runs through DELLA/ARF/ABI5 but those intermediate edges are absent from curated_edges.json. Exogenous-hormone in receptor-KO backgrounds will still modulate this target - a known Trap-5 framework limitation.]
Cytokinin induces CKX3 transcription as homeostatic feedback
CK binds AHK3 receptor
Activated AHK3 autophosphorylates and transfers phosphate to AHP1, which relays to nucleus
AHP1 phosphotransfer to ARR1 (type-B response regulator) activates its transcriptional activity in nucleus
Type-B ARRs are positive regulators of cytokinin growth responses; arr1 arr10 arr12 triple mutants have severe growth defects
ACS (ACC synthase) converts SAM to ACC, the rate-limiting step of ethylene biosynthesis
Ethylene binds ETR1 ER receptor and inactivates it; in the absence of ethylene, ETR1 is active and activates CTR1
ETR1 activates CTR1 kinase in the absence of ethylene, maintaining the off-state of ethylene signaling
CTR1 phosphorylates EIN2 C-terminus, sequestering it at the ER and preventing EIN3/EIL1 activation
Cleaved EIN2 C-terminus translocates to nucleus and stabilizes EIN3 by inhibiting EBF1/EBF2 F-box proteins that otherwise degrade EIN3
EIN3 propagates ethylene growth-inhibitory signal; EIN3 OE reduces stem elongation
NCED3 is the rate-limiting 9-cis-epoxycarotenoid dioxygenase producing xanthoxin, the ABA precursor
ABA binds PYR/PYL/RCAR receptors, inducing conformational change that enables PP2C inhibition
ABA-bound PYL physically blocks ABI1 (PP2C) phosphatase active site
ABI1 PP2C dephosphorylates and inactivates SnRK2 kinases in the absence of ABA
Active SnRK2 kinases phosphorylate and activate ABI5 and ABF bZIP transcription factors
ABI5 activates ABA-responsive growth-inhibitory genes; ABI5 OE reduces plant growth

ABA represses GA biosynthesis genes including GA20ox1 and GA3ox, contributing to ABA-GA antagonism in growth [Framework note: this edge bypasses a receptor Perception Gate (Motif 1). Biologically the feedback runs through DELLA/ARF/ABI5 but those intermediate edges are absent from curated_edges.json. Exogenous-hormone in receptor-KO backgrounds will still modulate this target - a known Trap-5 framework limitation.]
PIF4 activates YUCCA/auxin biosynthesis and cell wall remodeling genes to promote hypocotyl and stem elongation at warm temperature and in shade
Blue light activates FKF1 LOV domain, enabling GI interaction and CDF1 degradation
GI forms a complex with FKF1 in the afternoon of long days, required for FKF1 function
FKF1 directly interacts with CO and stabilizes it, preventing its degradation in the afternoon of long days
phyB promotes CO degradation in morning under red light
GI directly activates FT in parallel to CO
CO transcription factor directly binds the FT promoter and activates FT transcription in long-day afternoon
FCA is a nuclear RNA-binding protein that promotes proximal polyadenylation of COOLAIR (FLC antisense RNA), leading to chromatin silencing and repression of FLC sense transcription
VRN2 is a Su(z)12 homolog in PRC2 that H3K27me3-methylates FLC for long-term silencing
SPL9 directly binds to the promoter of MIR172B approximately 1.2 kb upstream of the transcriptional start site and activates its transcription
miR172 downregulates AP2 by translational repression (predominantly) rather than RNA cleavage
AP2 represses FT transcription; miR172 promotes flowering by relieving AP2 repression of FT
SVP directly represses FT transcription; svp mutants flower early
FLM (FLC-like MADS box) represses FT at low ambient temperature; FLM-SVP heterodimer is the active repressor
ELF3 is part of evening complex that binds PIF4 promoter to repress its transcription at dusk; also directly interacts with PIF4 protein
MAX3/CCD7 cleaves 9-cis-beta-carotene
SL suppresses axillary branching thereby channeling resources to main stem; SL-deficient mutants (max1/3/4) are bushy with shorter main stems
FT-FD complex activates SOC1 at the shoot apex
SOC1 promotes floral transition; reduces vegetative growth duration
Light-activated phyB excludes COP1 from the nucleus, stabilizing HY5
COP1 E3 ligase ubiquitinates HY5 for degradation in the dark; light inactivates COP1
HY5 bZIP transcription factor suppresses hypocotyl elongation by antagonizing PIF activity and activating photomorphogenic genes
TPS1 is the trehalose-6-phosphate synthase producing T6P
Trehalose-6-phosphate signaling in phloem companion cells induces FT expression during floral transition
T6P signaling represses miR156 in leaves to promote vegetative phase change
PP2A dephosphorylates BZR1, restoring its nuclear localization and DNA binding
SPINDLY (SPY) is an O-GlcNAc transferase that modifies DELLA proteins, positively regulating DELLA function/stability; spy mutations partially rescue GA deficiency
SL is hydrolyzed/perceived by D14 alpha/beta-hydrolase receptor, enabling D14-MAX2 interaction
SL-activated D14 interacts with MAX2 F-box as part of SCF complex targeting SMXLs
Strigolactone-activated MAX2 F-box ubiquitinates BES1 for degradation
phyA stabilizes CO protein in the afternoon under far-red light
ZTL directly interacts with CO and destabilizes it, opposing FKF1
CDF1 directly binds CO promoter to repress transcription in the morning of long days
FKF1-GI SCF complex ubiquitinates CDF1 for proteasome degradation, relieving CO repression
Cold-induced COOLAIR antisense lncRNA accelerates FLC transcriptional repression and reduces H3K36me3
LDL1 (LSD1-LIKE1) is an H3K4 demethylase that represses FLC expression redundantly with FLD; ldl1 mutants flower later than wild-type
